description.from:
## Problem
In , auto-follow/lock-to-bottom can pull the viewport back down after the user scrolls up in shorter conversations where the scrollable range is small.

## Expected behavior
- When user is effectively at the bottom, new messages auto-follow.
- As soon as user scrolls up any amount, auto-follow disables and does not fight manual scrolling.
- If user returns to bottom, auto-follow re-enables.

## Done when
- Scroll state is derived from a robust near-bottom check instead of a fixed-distance lock heuristic.
- Incoming messages only trigger scroll-to-bottom when follow mode is enabled.
- Manual upward scroll is respected immediately in both short and long conversations.
description.to:
## Problem
In `dashboard/components/ui/conversation-viewer.tsx`, auto-follow/lock-to-bottom can pull the viewport back down after the user scrolls up in shorter conversations where the scrollable range is small.

## Expected behavior
- When user is effectively at the bottom, new messages auto-follow.
- As soon as user scrolls up any amount, auto-follow disables and does not fight manual scrolling.
- If user returns to bottom, auto-follow re-enables.

## Done when
- Scroll state is derived from a robust near-bottom check instead of a fixed-distance lock heuristic.
- Incoming messages only trigger scroll-to-bottom when follow mode is enabled.
- Manual upward scroll is respected immediately in both short and long conversations.
